growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: bacdive-microa.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.method: consumption time course (Fig 4b); no numeric endpoint for wild-type M3 given

sec.method: HPLC of transformation products; conversion ratio 38% for parent strain

growth_records.cite: Su, Liqiu et al. Cofactor engineering to regulate NAD+/NADH ratio with its application to phytosterols biotransformation. Microbial Cell Factories (2017). DOI:10.1186/s12934-017-0796-4. [PMC5663084]
growth_records.snippet: The highly expressed NOX-2 in MNR M3N2 increased the conversion ratio (94%) when compared with that in the parent strain (38%)... all the substrate of phytosterols (5 g/L) was run out in MNR M3N2 fermentation at 144 h
growth_records.notes: Wild-type parent MNR M3. Biotransformation study: no specific growth rate, no biomass-normalized uptake/secretion rates reported; cell growth expressed as DCW (DCW mg/mL = 2.10 x protein mg/mL - 0.17, R2=0.99) but no rate. AD(D) conversion ratio 38% for wild-type parent (yield-like metric). Phytosterol consumption is a time course (Fig 4b) with no numeric endpoint for M3; cannot compute specific qS without biomass at timepoint. Temperature not stated (methods cite Shen et al. procedure). Product AD(D) = androst-4-ene-3,17-dione + androst-1,4-diene-3,17-dione.

growth_records.strain: TCCC 11978 MNR M3N2 (recombinant; pMV261-nox-2, water-forming NADH oxidase from Lactococcus lactis subsp. cremoris NZ9000)

up.method: volumetric: 5 g/L fully consumed by 144 h -> 5000 mg/L / 144 h

growth_records.cite: Su, Liqiu et al. Cofactor engineering to regulate NAD+/NADH ratio with its application to phytosterols biotransformation. Microbial Cell Factories (2017). DOI:10.1186/s12934-017-0796-4. [PMC5663084]
growth_records.snippet: The highly expressed NOX-2 in MNR M3N2 increased the conversion ratio (94%)... all the substrate of phytosterols (5 g/L) was run out in MNR M3N2 fermentation at 144 h. The highest AD (D) conversion ratio by MNR M3N2 was 94%
growth_records.notes: Recombinant strain (NADH oxidase nox from L. lactis over-expressed for NAD+ regeneration). Only explicitly derivable rate: volumetric phytosterol consumption = 5 g/L / 144 h = 0.0347 g/L/h (NOT biomass-normalized; no DCW at timepoint reported). AD(D) conversion ratio 94% (yield). No specific growth rate reported. Temperature not stated. Included as engineered-strain biotransformation record; qS specific rate not derivable.
